description: Key ETW providers that log offensive activities

providers.name: Microsoft-Windows-Kernel-Process
providers.offensive_relevance: Logs process creation, thread creation, process exit. Can detect unusual parent-child relationships and short-lived processes.
logged_activities: Process creation with full command line

providers.name: Microsoft-Windows-Threat-Intelligence
providers.offensive_relevance: High-fidelity detection of malicious patterns. Triggers on known attack techniques.
providers.evasion_notes: Hardest to evade. Uses ML and behavioral analysis.

providers.name: Microsoft-Windows-PowerShell
providers.offensive_relevance: Logs all PowerShell activity including script block content.
providers.evasion_notes: Script block logging captures everything. Use C# or alternative execution methods.

providers.name: Microsoft-Windows-DotNETRuntime
providers.offensive_relevance: Logs .NET assembly loading, JIT compilation, GC events.

providers.name: Microsoft-Windows-Kernel-Network
providers.offensive_relevance: Logs network connections with process context.

providers.name: Microsoft-Windows-Security-Auditing
providers.description: Security audit events (replaces Security event log)

providers.name: Microsoft-Windows-WMI-Activity
providers.offensive_relevance: WMI is heavily used for persistence and lateral movement.

providers.name: Microsoft-Windows-DNS-Client
providers.offensive_relevance: DNS tunneling, C2 domain resolution, DGA detection.

providers.name: Microsoft-Windows-RPC-Events
providers.offensive_relevance: RPC is used for lateral movement (WMI, DCOM, Services).

bypass_techniques.description: Patch EtwEventWrite in ntdll to prevent events from being written
bypass_techniques.detection_risk: High - modifying system DLLs is very suspicious

bypass_techniques.description: Unregister specific ETW providers to stop logging
bypass_techniques.detection_risk: High - requires admin privileges and is easily detected
bypass_techniques.implementation: Use EventUnregister API or direct registry manipulation

bypass_techniques.description: Use techniques that don't trigger high-fidelity providers
examples: Direct syscalls instead of Win32 APIs
Manual PE parsing instead of LoadLibrary
